source: Current Population Survey (CPS) sample used in Introductory Econometrics (Harvard University Press).
description: Cross-sectional dataset containing individual-level wage and demographic information, commonly used for econometrics examples involving wage determination, regression modeling, and demographic effects on earnings.

variables.region: Geographic region of residence (North or South).
variables.sex: Sex of the individual (Male or Female).

format.notes: Categorical variables should be converted to factors or dummy variables for modeling.

use_cases: Linear regression with factors and covariates
notes: Dataset contains observations from two regions: North and South.
Several categorical variables have multiple levels.
Wage values vary widely, useful for illustrating heteroskedasticity and leverage.
No missing values are present in the provided sample.
